summary: Phase 3 RETEST after critical bug fix. Backend: 25/25 tests PASSED sequentially. Frontend: register/login/duplicate-register/forgot-password/submit-clip-gate flows all working. The previous CRITICAL bug (sparse index null collision) is fully resolved — multiple distinct users now register cleanly. Index migration succeeded: telegram_id_unique_partial with partialFilterExpression={telegram_id:{$type:'string'}} verified in DB.

minor.issue: When user has no telegram_id, missing_channels returns [] (empty). Arguably should return REQUIRED_CHANNELS so frontend can pre-empt the gate before user clicks submit. Currently gate only triggers reactively via 403. Functionally OK but UX is sub-optimal.

TestSetPassword.test_set_password_flow flakes in parallel (xdist 2 workers) because module-scope cleanup fixture wipes shared DB users in one worker while another is still using them. Passes deterministically when -p no:xdist or run alone. Not a product bug — test isolation issue.

action_items: (OPTIONAL UX) Consider returning REQUIRED_CHANNELS in /auth/me missing_channels when user has no telegram_id, so FE can pre-empt gate before clicking submit-clip (currently gate only appears after 403 from POST /api/clips).

(OPTIONAL test infra) Make test_phase3_auth.py xdist-safe: either set scope='session' on cleanup with a worker-local UNIQUE_TAG, or pin tests to a single worker. Tests already pass cleanly sequentially.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L952-987 startup migration correctly drops legacy non-partial indexes on telegram_id and email before creating new partialFilterExpression indexes — verified live via mongosh.
server.py L683-690 DuplicateKeyError now correctly inspects keyPattern to distinguish username vs telegram_id duplicates.
server.py L394, L559, L654, L683 all User insert calls use model_dump(exclude_none=True) — null fields no longer materialize and trigger index collisions.
server.py is still 1005 lines; recommend splitting into routers (auth, telegram, clips, leaderboard).
validate_password_strength / validate_username / validate_phone are simple — could be combined into pydantic field validators on RegisterRequest/SetPasswordRequest for DRY.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase3_auth.py (updated TestIndexes.test_users_indexes to assert partialFilterExpression instead of legacy sparse)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all tested auth + gate flows

test_credentials: Pre-seeded: testuser_phase3 / sifre123. E2E user created during test (e2e_434817) — cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert pyt_ / e2e_ prefixed users + reset codes; module-scope teardown deletes them. Manual mongosh deleteMany ran for e2e_/pyt_/PYT* after frontend run — 0 leftover users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 fully green. mongosh confirms users indexes: username_1 (unique), telegram_id_unique_partial (unique + partialFilterExpression $type:string), email_partial (partial). Three+ distinct user registrations succeed back-to-back. Duplicate-username register correctly returns 409 with proper toast 'Bu kullanıcı adı zaten alınmış'. Submit-clip gate enforces server-side and FE renders gate-telegram-modal on 403. Forgot/reset endpoints OK. If running tests in CI, prefer -p no:xdist OR refactor cleanup to be xdist-safe.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration's CRITICAL bug (sparse index null collision) was correctly diagnosed and fixed by main agent. The fix: (1) drop legacy sparse telegram_id/email indexes on startup if they lack partialFilterExpression, (2) recreate them with partialFilterExpression={field:{$type:'string'}} so docs missing/null on that field are excluded from the unique index, (3) use model_dump(exclude_none=True) on every User insert so null fields don't materialize at all, (4) DuplicateKeyError handler now inspects e.details.keyPattern to return the correct conflict message. Verified live via mongosh + 25 pytest cases + end-to-end UI register flow with multiple distinct users.
